source.title: Scheduled tenant-scoped compliance bundles with residency-aware delivery
source.creator: Ava Chen
source.description:
Problem: Enterprise customers (SOC2/ISO-regulated) need scheduled, verifiable evidence bundles (PDF/CSV) that respect tenant data residency and retention policies. Current ad-hoc exports from the console are manual, region-agnostic, and do not include signed audit manifests or automatic retention-based pruning.

Scope: Provide a first-class scheduled export feature for the Console that produces time-boxed compliance bundles per tenant. Bundles must: (1) be scoped to tenant(s) and their residency setting, (2) obey retention windows (exclude records beyond tenant retention), (3) include a signed manifest (SHA256 checksums) and optional PGP signing, (4) support PDF summary + CSV raw exports, (5) be deliverable via region-aware S3 endpoints or secure download links, and (6) surface status in a new Compliance Exports dashboard with run history, success/failure reasons, and replay.

Out of scope for MVP: data transformation pipelines beyond CSV/PDF, calendar-linked on-prem delivery (separate Private deployment work), and long-term archival lifecycle management (handled by retention/archival SLA project).
acceptance_criteria: Admins can create scheduled export jobs in Console scoped to a tenant or tenant group with a recurrence (daily/weekly/monthly/custom cron).
Each export respects the tenant's data residency: export artifacts are written to region-specific S3 buckets or routed to the customer's configured storage endpoint in the tenant's region.
Exports apply tenant retention: records older than tenant.retention_days are excluded and noted in the manifest.
Artifacts include: (a) PDF executive summary with counts and SLO snapshots, (b) CSV of raw events/evidence, (c) signed manifest file with SHA256 checksums and optional PGP signature attachment.
Export jobs produce a run record visible in Compliance Exports dashboard with status, duration, size, and link to artifact location.
Failed runs provide actionable error codes and an automated retry policy with alerting to tenant owners.
Exports for tenants with residency set to 'EU' never cross into non-EU storage paths (operator policy enforced).
Download links are presigned and time-limited; audit record is created for each retrieval.
tasks: Write PRD: detailed schema for PDF summary and CSV columns (owner: Priya) - PRD link added to links
Design Console flow + dashboard mockups (owner: Noah) - get design review by 2025-11-20
API contract: /v1/compliance/exports create/list/cancel endpoints (owner: Luis) - include residency and retention params
Runtime: implement export worker with region routing (owner: ENG-2890) - include batching and incremental export support
Storage: set up per-region export buckets and operator policies (owner: Infra) - coordinate with security for kms keys
Signing: integrate manifest generation and optional PGP signing (owner: Security/Eng)
Telemetry: add metrics and traces for export jobs and retention exclusions (owner: Observability)
comments: 2025-11-04 - Ava Chen: Scoped initial PRD. Decided to support both PDF and CSV for MVP. Asked Eng to evaluate cost impact of daily exports for 1k tenants.

2025-11-12 - Luis Martinez (eng): Feasibility note: region routing requires per-region worker pools and buckets; will add a feature flag for operator-controlled region enforcement. Estimated infra work: 3 sprints.

2025-11-18 - Noah Patel (design): Uploaded Console mockups. Suggest inline job preview and last-run card on tenant page. Flagged small UX trade-off: single unified job vs. per-tenant-per-template jobs. Prefers per-template for clarity.

2025-12-01 - Security (Priya + Martin): Requested PGP signing option and manifest checksum requirement. Also requested KMS envelope encryption for stored artifacts. Marked ticket for security review in SEC-88.

2026-01-07 - Priya Singh (assignee): Kickoff with Eng; agreed API contract and manifest schema. Added acceptance criteria for retention exclusions and audit retrieval logs. Beginning implementation of background worker.

2026-02-20 - QA (Samira): Early integration tests show failure when tenant retention is 0 (special case). Added task to ensure inclusive semantics for 0-day retention (means immediate deletion - exports should be empty but still generate manifest).
2026-03-10 - Priya Singh: Implementation branch merged to staging (GH PR 3412). Dashboard skeleton wired to export job API. Next: harden signed URL expiry and add replay/run history pagination.

source.title: Billing intake lifecycle and decision heuristics for enterprise pricing requests
source.creator: Aisha Raman
source.description: Purpose: Define a repeatable intake-to-resolution lifecycle and a decision heuristic for non-bug customer requests about pricing, commitments, invoicing, cost allocation, and marketplace billing. This ticket captures the operational playbook that will be used by Sales/CS/Early Customers, Growth, and Product to triage and route requests so we can: 1) reduce time-to-decision, 2) apply consistent commercial outcomes, and 3) surface engineering cost and legal constraints early.
acceptance_criteria: A single intake form template for pricing/commitment/invoicing requests that collects commercial, technical, and legal signals
A scoring rubric that can be applied at Triage to decide routing (Product backlog vs Billing Eng vs Enterprise Ops vs Marketplace team)
SLA definitions for initial response and routing with clear owners for each pathway
Example routing decisions for 6 representative customer asks (commitment tiers, invoice delivery requirements, marketplace fee split, retroactive credits)
A lightweight dashboard query in the intake tracker to show pending triage items and mean time to route
intake_form_fields: Requestor name and company (name, account ID)
Type of request: commitments | invoice | cost-allocation | marketplace-listing | marketplace-fee | pricing-exception
Business driver / ask in one sentence
Desired commercial outcome (discount, billed promise, reclassification) and duration
Expected monthly ARR impact (estimate) and contract length
Is this tied to a Sales/Legal/Finance approval? (yes/no + contact)
Technical constraints (VPC/on-prem, billing export format, invoice cadence)
Customer tier (self-serve | mid-market | enterprise) and existing commitments
Attachments: contract excerpt, marketplace terms, screenshot of billing report
triage_rubric: Impact score (0-5): revenue at stake, renewal risk, legal/regulatory exposure
Complexity score (0-5): engineering work, accounting changes, billing pipeline updates
Strategic score (0-5): marketplace strategic partner, reference customer, new region/regulation
Urgency multiplier: 1 (normal), 1.5 (sales-led with close date <30d), 2 (compliance/legal require immediate action)
Routing thresholds: if Impact>=4 AND (Urgency multiplier>=1.5 OR Strategic>=3) => route to Product + Sales Leadership for commercial decision
If Complexity>=3 AND requires schema/invoice changes => route to Billing Engineering (ENG-BILL) with Product as companion owner
If Marketplace-specific (fee split, listing) => route to Marketplace Ops + Legal
If small self-serve pricing exceptions (Impact<=2 AND Complexity<=2) => Growth/PLG to own temporary clampdown or promo mechanics
routing_matrix: Product backlog: pricing model changes, commitment tier productization, new SKU definitions
Billing Engineering: invoice format changes, export pipeline, revenue-recognition affects, retroactive credits implementation
Enterprise Ops / Customer Success: contract-only or SSO/billing contact updates, one-off invoicing cadence adjustments
Legal / Finance: changes that affect terms, tax handling, marketplace contract templates
Growth / PLG: promotional discounts, marketplace promotional placements, self-serve coupons
source.sla: Initial response to intake: 2 business days. Triage decision & route: 5 business days. Estimated resolution timeline communicated within 10 business days for most cases; large engineering work items will get a scoped PRD and release milestone.
operational_playbook: Step 1: Intake submitted via Pricing Intake Form (Slack shortcut or Zendesk tag)
Step 2: Intake team (Product Ops) validates completeness within 2 business days
Step 3: Apply triage rubric and assign routing owner(s); document decision rationale in intake tracker
Step 4: If engineering is required, create ENG ticket with scope, attach contract excerpts and accounting impact
Step 5: Schedule commercial review (if Impact>=4) with Sales Leadership + Finance within 3 business days
Step 6: Publish temporary workaround or policy note for internal teams if resolution will exceed 2 weeks
Step 7: Close intake with final disposition and update customer-facing owner (CS or Sales)
Step 8: Post-mortem for any escalated/prioritized items that change platform billing behavior
example_decisions: Case A (Enterprise commitment request): Customer requests 12-month committed spend with invoicing monthly and custom cost-center allocation. Impact=5, Complexity=3 => Route: Product + Billing Eng + Finance. Outcome: PRD for commitment SKU, billing export enhancement, Legal amendment template.
Case B (Invoice format change for a single account): Impact=2, Complexity=3 => Route: Billing Eng + CS. Outcome: One-off invoice exporter job and playbook for manual delivery until automation ship.

Case C (Marketplace fee split dispute from partner): Impact=4, Complexity=2, Strategic=4 => Route: Marketplace Ops + Legal + Product. Outcome: Partner-facing contract addendum and fee settlement process.
Case D (Small pricing exception request from self-serve): Impact=1, Complexity=1 => Route: Growth. Outcome: Promo code and customer communications owned by Growth.
next_steps: Validate intake form fields with Sales/CS by Mar 17
Prototype intake workflow in Notion + Slack integration for automated reminders by Mar 24
Run 2-week pilot where all new billing/pricing requests go through this intake flow (measure MTTR & routing accuracy)
Request Billing Eng to prepare a template ENG-BILL ticket with required accounting fields by Mar 20
comments: 2026-02-12 Aisha Raman: Initial draft of rubric posted. Want feedback from Billing Eng on required fields for engineering tickets.
2026-02-18 Priya Nair: Added requirement for revenue-impact field and GL code mapping when Complexity>=3.
2026-02-25 Lydia Chen: Sales requests that 'go-live' date be captured as a hard requirement for any sales-led asks.
2026-03-03 Mateo Alvarez: Updated routing thresholds to include strategic multiplier. Moved pilot start to Mar 24 to align with Sales team availability.
2026-03-09 Helena Schultz: CS requests a template customer communication for deferred resolutions; added to next_steps.

source.title: Stability Fingerprint & Actionable Observability for Model Rollouts
source.creator: Ava Chen
source.description: Build a 'Stability Fingerprint' experience in Console that surfaces per-cohort health and causality signals for canary rollouts, A/B tests, and shadow traffic. The feature bundles cohort-level dashboards, side-by-side metric diffs, trace links, per-route distributions (latency, p99, error rates), and automated recommendations for safe actions (pause, ramp, rollback). It should integrate with the rollout policy engine to enable threshold-based auto-rollback and dry-run modes, and provide explainable signal-attribution so engineers can quickly determine whether regressions are model-quality, infra, or traffic-related.
acceptance_criteria: Console shows a Stability Fingerprint card per rollout variant that summarizes key signals (latency, errors, quality divergence, throughput) with visual severity bands
Side-by-side cohort diff view highlights statistically significant deltas and links each delta to trace samples and example inputs
Per-route metric explorer allows filtering by route, model variant, and time-window; supports sampling of KV cache hit ratio and batching changes
Auto-rollback policy editor supports threshold-based triggers, scope (route vs global), cooldowns, and dry-run; policies can be attached to rollouts
Recommendations panel surfaces top 3 likely causes with suggested next steps and linked runbooks/PRs
End-to-end tests cover canary fail, shadow divergence, and A/B regression scenarios; telemetry validated against staging dashboards
Performance: Dashboard queries for a 6-hour window with 10M tokens complete within 800ms for typical queries
updates: 2026-03-01: PRD created — initial goals and success metrics (link: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ROLLOUTS/Stability+Fingerprint+PRD)
2026-03-04: Design sync — explored compact snapshot + expandable forensic panel; Figma draft shared (link: https://www.figma.com/file/abcd1234/stability-fingerprint)
2026-03-07: Eng spike found missing cohort-level KV cache telemetry; ENG assigned owner to implement cohort-tagged metrics (ENG-87412)
2026-03-09: UX review with SEs — requested quick jump-to-trace and example-requests in diff rows
2026-03-10: Backend schema change merged for cohort ids and sample pointers (GH PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/console/pull/4567)
2026-03-10: Added auto-rollback dry-run mode to scope for early QA
review_notes: PM review (2026-03-05): tighten definition of 'quality divergence' to include embedding drift and token-level perplexity
Design review (2026-03-09): favor compact fingerprint with drilldowns rather than full-width diffs by default
Engineering review (2026-03-07): require sampling API for example inputs to avoid PII exposure
source.notes: MVP focuses on highest-impact signals for production rollouts: latency/p99, error-rate, top-k model-quality regressions (from evals), and KV cache hit ratio. Longer-term: integrate with Optimize to recommend quantization/variant fallbacks. Coordinate with Security for sample redaction guidelines before shipping. QA plan includes synthetic regression runs and staged canary in Dedicated environment.

source.title: Field enablement kit: objection-flow maps, demo prompts, rollback playbooks
source.creator: Priya Khanna
source.description: Create a compact, field-ready enablement kit for Sales and CS to surface runtime-1.20 features (Latency-Aware Routing, adaptive prefix caching improvements, and canary rollout controls). Deliverables: concise battlecard (2-page), objection-response flow maps, 5 scenario demo scripts (API+Console), quick rollback/runbook snippets for common incidents, and a coordinated release-notes summary for CS Slack and release email. Coordinate review cycles with Engineering, Support, Legal, and Docs.
tasks: Draft 2-page battlecard: key TL;DR, 3 value props, top objections and rebuttals (owner: PM)
Build 5 scenario demo scripts: Quickstart API, private deployment demo, dedicated autoscale case, SLO recovery, and rollback flow (owner: Solutions Eng)
Create objection-flow maps (visuals) and 3 short talk tracks for AE use (owner: Design + PM)
Prepare short CS playbook: triage checklist, escalation path, canned responses and runbook snippets for common incidents (owner: Support)
Coordinate release-notes excerpt and CS/AE Slack template; finalize messaging with Legal and Security (owner: PM)
Run 1-hour rehearsal with Sales & CS and collect feedback (owner: PM)
Publish final assets to sales docs hub and notify enablement channel (owner: PM)
Add 'rollback snippet' code examples to docs (owner: Engineering)
Capture demo recording and create 3 short clips for quick playback in demos (owner: Design)
Post-launch monitor: compile first-week feedback and open follow-up tickets (owner: PM)
acceptance_criteria: Battlecard completed and approved by AE lead and CS lead
All 5 demo scripts recorded or validated in a dry run
CS playbook reviewed by Support with at least 2 canned responses added
Legal and Security have signed off on customer-facing release notes blurb
Assets uploaded to Sales docs hub and announcement templates published
Rehearsal completed with attendees from Sales, CS, Solutions Eng, and two action items captured for follow-up
comments: 2026-02-12 Priya K.: Kickoff — scoped deliverables with AE lead (Marisol), Support (Diego), and Eng owners. Emphasis on translating latency improvements into customer-facing ROI language.
2026-02-18 Ethan P.: Draft battlecard v0 posted to Drive. Key value props pulled from PRD; need tighter cost/latency numbers from Eng for accuracy.
2026-02-20 Diego S. (Support): Added triage checklist skeleton and suggested escalation paths; flagged need for rollback snippets linked to runtime rollback-CLI PR.
2026-02-25 Eng Sync: Engineering provided latency/throughput delta table vs runtime-1.19. Added canary percent defaults and fallback behavior notes. Document link added to artifacts.
2026-03-02 Design: Uploaded objection-flow visuals; recommend simplifying to 3 paths for AE use. Asked PM to prioritize top 5 objections for copy.
2026-03-05 Legal: Reviewed release blurb; requested removal of forward-looking revenue claims and added approved phrasing for 'beta' features.
2026-03-07 Rehearsal scheduled for 2026-03-12. Sales and CS reps confirmed. Action: record inputs from roleplay to refine talk tracks.

2026-03-12 Rehearsal notes (Priya): Roleplay uncovered two unclear handoffs between Support and Eng during rollback. Created follow-up ticket ENG-76542 to add a small endpoint to surface rollback status.
2026-03-10 Ethan P.: Updated final drafts with rehearsal feedback; preparing distribution plan and Slack templates. Set publication for 2026-03-18.
2026-03-10 Priya K.: Security requested a one-line note about KMS key handling for private deployments — added to CS playbook. Security sign-off attached in comments.
source.rollout_notes: Publish assets to Sales Docs hub on 2026-03-18 09:00 PT, send AE+CS Slack blast with pinned meeting recording and 2 short clips. Include 'Known issues' section and immediate steps for rollback. Coordinate with On-call rota for first 72 hours.
demo_script_examples: API Quickstart (2-min): Create client, call /v1/generate with 'latency_profile=low' and compare token-per-second numbers. Talk track: 'For low-latency scenarios we route into LAR profiles to prioritize p99. Results: typical p95 drops by ~40% vs default.'

Private deployment walk-through (4-min): Show VPC setup, KMS key selection, and private control plane toggle in Console. Talk track: 'Private gives you full network control and explicit KMS keys for compliance.'

Dedicated autoscale scenario (3-min): Trigger load, show dashboard throughput and autoscale event, then show fallback to compatible model variant. Talk track: 'Dedicated pools can burst with predictable latency; policy ensures graceful fallback.'

SLO recovery demo (3-min): Simulate degraded node, show automatic routing to canary with traffic shift and then seamless failback. Talk track: 'Canary routing is weight-based and reversible — minimal application disruption.'

Rollback playbook (2-min): Run CLI snippet to pin previous model version, verify traffic shift, and post a short status update to CS channel. Talk track: 'Fast rollback is a safety valve; we've codified the few steps required to restore pre-release behavior.'

source.title: Cohort Storyboard + Rollback Policy Editor for rollout observability
source.creator: Priya Kothari
source.description:
Problem: Engineers and SREs running canary/A-B/ shadow rollouts lack a single, narrative-driven view that ties per-route metrics, request traces, and cohort diffs together with an editable auto-rollback policy. Current Console panels surface metric tables and simple rollouts, but teams spend manual effort correlating trace samples, error fingerprints, and traffic-slice differences before deciding to roll back.

Goal: Build a "Cohort Storyboard" in Console — a timeline-first, side-by-side comparison that links cohorts to sample traces, per-route token/latency/cost breakdowns, and a visual rollback policy editor (thresholds, hysteresis, and staged actions). The feature should also enable hypothesis annotations and exportable snapshots for postmortems.

Scope: MVP focuses on HTTP/text-generation routes and the most common rollout types: canary (percent-based), A/B (split), and shadow. Integrations: runtime telemetry (per-route metrics), tracing service (sample link + context), and rollout control plane for applying policy-driven auto-rollbacks or fast-fallbacks.

Out of scope for MVP: full cohort statistical testing engine, UI for multi-model cascades, and on-prem only features (will follow in Private deployment phase).
acceptance_criteria: Console shows a timeline view with at least two cohorts side-by-side for a given rollout
Each cohort row links to 3 representative traces (latency, error, and token-profile) with a one-click open-to-tracing UI
Per-route token cost, p50/p95 latency, error-rate, and inference throughput are shown and downloadable as CSV
A visual rollback policy editor exists: can set metric, threshold, and action (pause/rollback/route-shift) and save as a named policy
Auto-rollback executes on a canary when policy conditions hold and writes an audit entry visible in the rollout timeline
Snapshots and annotations can be exported to Drive/Confluence (link attached) for postmortem
Feature passes security review and has RBAC gating for rollback actions
milestones: Backend telemetry endpoints and cohort-diff API contract (ENG) (2026-03-18)
Frontend prototype + usability test with 3 early customers (2026-03-28)
Beta release to internal teams and 2 early customers (2026-04-10)
progress_updates: 2026-02-26: Kickoff sync with Eng (Mateo), Design (Lena), and SRE (Aisha). Agreed to reuse existing per-route telemetry APIs and extend cohort metadata. Action: ENG to propose API contract by 2026-03-04.

2026-03-04: ENG API contract posted: github.com/redwood-inference/telemetry/blob/feature/cohort-storyboard (PR #412). Includes cohort-level aggregates and example sample-trace payloads. Trade-off: returning 3 representative traces per cohort increases trace storage lookups; Eng proposes a 1-second sampling window to bound cost.

2026-03-06: Design critique: prioritized timeline-first UX over heatmap-first. Design decision documented in Confluence: https://redwood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/infra/pages/COHORT-STORYBOARD-WIREFRAMES. Follow-up: add explicit hysteresis UI to rollback editor after privacy review.

2026-03-08: Security review kickoff: identified need for RBAC check on rollback actions and audit log entry. Security requested KMS key usage for policy exports. Product to create a short brief for compliance by 2026-03-11.

2026-03-10: Prototype usability test scheduled with Platform team and 2 pilot customers (FinLedger, ChatDesk). Initial feedback: customers want ability to pin a cohort snapshot and compare non-consecutive windows — adding as stretch goal.
risks: Representative trace selection could bias investigation if anomaly windows are narrow
Telemetry cardinality increase if cohort diffs request full per-route histograms; ENG may limit to aggregated buckets
Automatic rollback could trigger unnecessary rollbacks without sufficient hysteresis — must expose safe defaults and require RBAC to enable auto actions
open_questions: Should auto-rollback be opt-in per team or per-project? (security prefers opt-in by default)
What is the canonical 'representative trace' algorithm? (top error, median latency, and highest token cost are candidates)
Do we support export of snapshots to S3 in addition to Drive/Confluence?
next_steps: ENG to finalize cohort-diff API contract and add sampling guardrails (owner: Mateo, due: 2026-03-12)
Design to produce high-fidelity mockups of the rollback policy editor and timeline interactions (owner: Lena, due: 2026-03-15)
Product to draft customer-facing runbook and enablement doc for beta (owner: Priya, due: 2026-03-22)
Schedule security artifact review and RBAC gating tests with Security (owner: Aisha, due: 2026-03-11)

source.title: Custom SLA Profiles + Eval-Gated Cutover Playbook for Hosted Endpoints
source.creator: Ava Chen
source.summary: Define and deliver customizable per-tenant SLA profiles for hosted chat/completions/embeddings/rerank endpoints, gated by eval-driven telemetry and an automated cutover playbook that supports partial rollbacks and safe fallbacks.
source.background: Customers increasingly demand contractual latency and quality guarantees that vary by plan. Current hosted endpoints have a single behavior profile and manual rollout procedures. We need a productized way to offer configurable SLA profiles (latency/throughput/quality tiers), tie them to telemetry/eval gates, and provide an operational runbook that minimizes risk during launch and subsequent changes.
source.problem_statement: We lack a robust product and operational workflow to (1) expose configurable SLA profiles to customers, (2) validate them against quality/eval signals before changing traffic, and (3) automate safe cutovers with partial rollback and policy-based fallbacks to ensure uptime and cost targets.
goals_success_criteria: Ship SLA Profiles UI + API for defining per-tenant latency and quality bands
Integrate eval-run gating so a rollout is blocked if quality regressions exceed threshold on canary traffic
Automated cutover playbook that can execute progressive traffic ramp, pause, or rollback based on telemetry
Operational dashboards show SLA adherence per tenant within 1 minute of violation
Reduce manual rollout incidents for hosted endpoints by 70% in first quarter post-launch
scope_in_scope: SLA profile model (latency, p50/p95, availability, quality band) and API
Console UI for tenant SLA creation and preview
Backend enforcement hooks that tag tenant traffic with SLA profile
Eval-driven gating integration with pre-existing eval service
Cutover orchestration scripts (canary -> ramp -> full) with automated rollback triggers
Acceptance tests and runbook for support/oncall
scope_out_of_scope: Billing or legal contract automation for SLA credits
Dedicated/Dedicated-reserved capacity changes (this project focuses on hosted lanes only)
Full RBAC redesign — will use existing RBAC surfaces
requirements: API: create/update/delete SLA profiles (JSON schema) with fields: name, latency_targets (p50/p95), availability_target, quality_thresholds (eval metric + delta), rollout_policy_id
UI: SLA profile editor with preview traffic impact and estimated cost delta
Runtime: tag each request with tenant_sla_id and expose to routing layer
Eval: run evaluation suite against canary traffic with pass/fail thresholds
Orchestration: implement progressive rollout engine supporting step durations, percent steps, and automated rollback based on alerts
Telemetry: per-tenant SLA metrics emitted to metrics backend and aggregated in Console with 1-min granularity
acceptance_criteria: Create SLA profile via API and UI; persisted and visible in tenant settings
Canary rollout to 1% traffic runs evals; if eval delta > threshold, rollout halts and system posts annotated incident
SLA metrics (p50, p95, availability, eval score) show expected values in Console within 60s of collection
Automated rollback executes to previous stable model/variant when SLA breach triggers during ramp
Runbook validated in a simulated dry-run with oncall team and incident checklist completed
E2E test coverage for critical paths (API create/update, orchestration triggers, rollback)
rollout_checklist: Pre-launch: finalize SLA schema and UI mocks; legal review for wording
Pre-launch: ENG implement runtime tagging and routing hook; add unit/integration tests
Pre-launch: EVAL team author tenant-specific probe suites and baseline datasets
Pre-launch: Deploy telemetry dashboards and alerting rules for SLA thresholds
Day-0: Start with internal tenants with conservative SLAs and 0.5% traffic canary
Day-1: Broaden to pilot customers (5%) after passing eval gates for 24h
Day-3: Ramp to 25% if no quality regressions and latency targets met
Day-7: Reach full production for trial tier with monitoring checks green
Cutover: runbook owner logs each step, takes annotated snapshot of key metrics, and ensures rollback window open
Post-launch: 30-day monitoring window and weekly retro to surface gaps
telemetry_and_metrics: SLO alert thresholds: p95_latency > configured_target * 1.2 for 3 consecutive 1-min buckets -> page oncall
Dashboard: SLA card per tenant with current status and last 24h trends
risks_and_mitigations: Risk: Eval suites may be brittle for some customers -> Mitigation: include fallback gating on golden prompt subset and require manual review for small-volume tenants
Risk: Incorrectly configured SLA causes over-provisioning -> Mitigation: add cost-estimator in UI and default conservative profiles
Risk: Rollback automation flips between variants rapidly -> Mitigation: add cooldown window and max-rollback-rate throttle
Risk: Telemetry lag hides violations -> Mitigation: instrument direct metrics export path and alert on telemetry-health
open_questions: Should SLA profiles allow hard cap on token spend per minute?
What is the legal team’s minimum acceptance wording for ‘availability’ vs observed uptime?
Do we expose SLA templates for different industry verticals (finance, healthcare)?
How should cross-tenant burst isolation interact with SLA enforcement?
stakeholders: Engineering: Marcus Rivera (backend lead), Priya Patel (runtime)
source.notes: This PRD focuses on hosted endpoints only and is written to enable a phased launch tied to evaluation telemetry. For Dedicated/private offerings we will adapt the SLA model but surface is out-of-scope for this ticket. Include customer success in pilot onboarding to capture edge-case prompts for eval suites.

title: Custom SLA Profiles + Eval-Gated Cutover Playbook for Hosted Endpoints
preview: Define and deliver customizable per-tenant SLA profiles for hosted chat/completions/embeddings/rerank endpoints, gated by eval-driven telemetry and an automated cutover playbook that supports partial rollbacks and safe fallbacks.
